data.title: Why Investors Dumped Billions from China ETFs in 6 Months
data.description:
Investors have withdrawn billions from China-focused ETFs (exchange-traded funds) amid economic disappointments and policy headwinds. Disappointing post-COVID growth, escalating U.S.-China trade tensions under President Trump, including steep tariffs and delisting risks, drove massive outflows from funds. Government signals to cool AI-fueled rallies, coupled with shifts to higher-return options like India ETFs, further accelerated the exodus.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Using TipRanks' Compare ETFs Tool, we identified iShares MSCI China ETF (MCHI), iShares China Large-Cap ETF (FXI), and Xtrackers Harvest CSI 300 China A-Shares ETF (ASHR) as three ETFs that have witnessed high net outflows over the past six months. Combined, investors have pulled more than $1 billion from these three funds.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: 'We Shall See,' Says Top Investor About Archer Aviation Stock
data.description:
Archer Aviation (NYSE:ACHR) hasn't exactly been flying high over the past few months, and its share price has been trending downward in the new year. Would the eVTOL operator's Q4 and full-year 2025 numbers help things pick back up?

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

So far, the answer appears to be a resounding "no." ACHR has slipped almost 10% since unveiling its most recent numbers earlier this week.

The news wasn't all bad, however.

The company demonstrated regulatory progress, announcing that it had received Federal Aviation Administration acceptance for "Means of Compliance." In plain English, this signifies the company and the FAA have agreed on the means to demonstrate the airworthiness of the company's Midnight aircraft.

Moreover, the company also displayed a record level of liquidity of close to $2.0 billion. For those worried about the company's ability to keep the lights on in the years ahead, this funding provides a concrete demonstration that Archer has the means to continue on its path for the foreseeable future.

And yet, the concerns surrounding the company's profitability (or lack thereof) aren't going away. The company missed on EPS projections by -$0.02, clocking in a loss of -$0.26 per share.

While losing money isn't exactly celebrated, this is an improvement over its EPS of -$0.45 in Q4 2024. However, it's not that straightforward, according to top investor Rich Smith.

"Investors might have been pleased by the number except for one thing: How Archer made its losses shrink," explains the 5-star investor, who is among the top 4% of stock pros covered by TipRanks.

After all, the company's record liquidity resulted from equity-diluting capital raises. Smith points out that the company's net loss of $189 million this past quarter didn't improve significantly from the $198 million it tallied a year ago.

"The reason the per-share loss shrank so much was that there were so many more shares this time," the investor states.

Moreover, the dilution could come back to bite investors in the future, adds Smith, as they'll have less claim to the company's eventual profits (if Archer does succeed in turning the corner).

With that in mind, is ACHR a good investment? The investor points out that most analysts don't expect Archer to become profitable before 2030. Count Smith among those are just not sure about ACHR.

"We shall see," he concludes. (To watch Rich Smith's track record, click here)

Wall Street is looking at the glass half full, however. With 4 Buys and 2 Holds, ACHR rises to a Moderate Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $13.25 points to gains approaching 100% in the year ahead. (See ACHR stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Berkshire Hathaway Stock (BRK.B) Jumps as CEO Greg Abel Commits His Salary to Share Purchases
data.description:
Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) stock rallied on Thursday after CEO Greg Abel said he plans to use all of his annual salary to purchase shares of the investment company's stock. That's a major commitment from Abel, as his take-home pay is set at roughly $15 million per year. His on-paper salary is $25 million, but that doesn't account for taxes.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

To back up his claim, Abel purchased $15.3 million worth of BRK.A stock, which comes to 21 shares. These were bought for roughly $730,000 each. This news has Abel following in the footsteps of former Berkshire Hathaway CEO and current Chairman Warren Buffett, who also invested a significant portion of his wealth in the company's shares.

Abel also noted that Berkshire Hathaway will restart share repurchases, which have been on hold for the past six quarters. He said, "We will buy back Berkshire shares when they trade below our estimate of intrinsic value, conservatively determined, ensuring that repurchases enhance per-share value for continuing owners. We may also purchase large blocks of shares directly from major holders when the opportunity presents itself. These purchases allow shareholders to own an incrementally larger piece of Berkshire's businesses, without deploying any additional capital of their own."

Berkshire Hathaway Stock Movement Today

Berkshire Hathaway stock was up on Thursday alongside the CEO share purchases. This pushed shares of BRK.B stock 0.82% higher this morning, following a 1.27% rally yesterday. However, the stock was still down 3.02% year-to-date and 2.08% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: ChatGPT Could Soon Be on WhatsApp after Meta's EU Climbdown
data.description:
Shares in Meta Platforms (META) looked less clever today as it said it was opening up WhatsApp to rival AI chatbots – potentially including OpenAI's ChatGPT – in the face of a possible antitrust slap-down from the EU.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meta said AI rivals would be allowed on the WhatsApp platform for a year.

EU Threats Shake Meta

This followed threats from the European Commission, the EU competition enforcer, that it would introduce interim measures to prevent potential serious and irreparable harm to rivals after Meta blocked them from WhatsApp.

Meta will let rival AI chatbots access WhatsApp for a fee. The company barred them on January 15, allowing only its Meta AI assistant on the service.

"For the next 12 months, we'll support general purpose AI chatbots using the WhatsApp Business API in Europe in response to the European Commission's regulatory process," a Meta spokesperson said. "We believe that this removes the need for any immediate intervention as it gives the European Commission the time it needs to conclude its investigation."

The Commission said it was analysing how Meta's changes might affect both its interim measures review and its broader antitrust investigation.

Too Much Strain

Meta has previously said the rise of chatbots on its platforms strains its systems and that other channels exist for AI providers, including app stores, search engines, email services, partnership integrations and operating systems.

Meta allowed rival chatbots onto WhatsApp in Italy in January after an order from the Italian antitrust authority, which is still investigating.

An upgrade to the messaging platform last October meant the only AI assistant available to use on WhatsApp was Meta AI, the agent developed by the US tech group, which also owns Facebook and Instagram.

The European Commission said Meta was the dominant player for messaging in the EU market and was "abusing" this position by "refusing access to WhatsApp to other businesses".

It said it "considers that WhatsApp is an important entry point" for AI chatbots such as OpenAI's ChatGPT, to "reach consumers".

Is META a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, META has a Strong Buy consensus based on 39 Buy and 5 Hold ratings. Its highest price target is $1,144. META stock's consensus price target is $858.86, implying a 28.62% upside.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Why Is Classover Stock (KIDZ) Down Today?
data.description:
Classover (KIDZ) stock took a beating on Thursday after the education technology company announced a reverse stock split. The company said that it will consolidate shares on a one-for-50 basis, boosting the price of its stock while lowering its number of outstanding shares. The shares will continue to trade under the KIDZ ticker after the reverse split goes into effect on March 10, 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Classover said that the reverse stock split is required to maintain its listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market (NDAQ). This is due to the current price of the shares being below the $1 minimum bid price required to maintain its listing. A reverse stock split allows the company to increase its share price and avoid a delisting notice from the exchange.

Classover noted that the reverse stock split will also affect its warrants. This will change the exercise price of these warrants and the number of shares that they will grant.

Classover Stock Movement Today

Classover stock was down 27.68% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a 17.48% rally yesterday. The shares have fallen 46.01% year-to-date and 99.07% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of KIDZ stock, as more than 11 million shares changed hands. For comparison, the company's three-month daily average trading volume is about 3.56 million units.

It makes sense that KIDZ stock would drop on today's news. A reverse split to boost its share price calls into question the company's stability and hurts investor morale.
data.author: William White

data.title: 'Demand Is Strong Enough': Five-Star Analyst Bullish on Iren's $3.5B Capex for Nvidia GPUs, Other Hardware
data.description:
Shares in AI cloud company Iren Limited (IREN) plunged early Thursday despite its new purchase agreement with Nvidia (NVDA). H.C. Wainwright's five-star analyst Mike Colonnese is bullish on the arrangement, noting that the Australian company's plan to spend $3.5 billion on hardware during the second half of this year shows that "customer demand is strong enough."

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Iren Agrees to Buy 50,000 Nvidia GPUs

On Wednesday, Iren said it was expanding its fleet to 150,000 graphics processing units (GPUs) by buying 50,000 of Nvidia's next-generation B300 data center GPUs.

In addition to the GPUs, the Sydney-based company plans to commit its $3.5 billion of capital expenditure (capex) on servers, storage, and networking equipment during the last six months of this year.

Analyst Flags Bullish Cues in Iren's Latest Update

Reacting to the update, Colonnese reaffirmed his Buy rating on IREN stock and maintained his price target of $80, suggesting over 82% upside.

The analyst noted that the move showed management confidence in solid customer demand, as the transactions are planned ahead of any secured contracts.

'An Important Step Forward'

Furthermore, Colonnese believes that the transaction signals to the market that Iren is on schedule in terms of the progress of its under-construction data center infrastructure in Childress, Texas, and Mackenzie, British Columbia.

"Netting it all out, investors should view yesterday's announcement as an important step forward in IREN derisking its fiscal 2026 outlook for the AI Cloud business," the analyst noted.

He added that Iren's raised guidance is another pointer to the management's conviction in the firm's capacity to ramp up its AI operations.

How Can Iren Reverse Falling Shares?

In terms of investors' reaction to the update — Iren's shares were down over 6% to $41.20 as of 8:28 a.m. EST– Colonnese blamed the selloff on concerns about share dilution sparked by the company's plans to potentially sell up to $6 billion in new shares.

To turn things around, the analyst contended, Iren will need to show that it can lock in attractive financing for its uncontracted hardware.

Is IREN Stock a Good Buy?

Across Wall Street, analysts' approach to IREN's shares remains tempered, with the consensus rating on the stock presently a Moderate Buy. This breaks down into eight Buys, two Holds, and one Sell recommendation over the past three months.

However, the average IREN price target of $79.78 implies about 82% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Sell Rivian (RIVN) Shares Because Its Expectations Are Too High, Says BofA
data.description:
Rivian (NASDAQ:RIVN) investors are hoping the release of its more affordable mid-size SUV, the R2, will be a game-changer for the company, but it is unlikely to have the desired effect.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That is the opinion of BofA analyst Alexander Perry, who believes the electric vehicle maker will find it hard to gain traction in the current environment.

Perry anticipates that EV adoption will remain relatively modest, which could limit near-term profit growth amid a challenging regulatory backdrop, including the expiration of federal tax credits and the rollback of CAFÉ and greenhouse gas standards that previously supported regulatory credit revenue. Even under a more supportive regulatory framework, Perry believes U.S. consumer uptake of EVs has lagged expectations due to concerns over affordability, driving range, charging availability, and low resale values.

Perry sees overall EV penetration falling to 6% of light vehicle sales in 2026, down from 8% in 2025, due to the phase-out of EV incentive programs and a reduced focus on EVs by traditional automakers, particularly Ford and General Motors. The analyst's forecast implies a more than 20% drop in industry EV sales, contrasting with Rivian's call for a 53% increase in deliveries (including the R2 model, flat if excluding it), which would suggest market share gains while competitors shift toward higher-margin internal combustion engine vehicles and scale back premium EV programs.

Perry does think the R2 launch will broaden Rivian's addressable market, noting that roughly 69% of U.S. EVs sold in 2025 were priced under $50K, or about 845,000 units. That said, competition in this price segment is intensifying, with Tesla introducing more affordable Model 3/Y standard variants ($37K/$40K), priced 13%/11% below their original models, Lucid planning a mid-size platform under $50K in 2027 – 37% below its current Gravity Platform – and Ford aiming for more affordable EVs built on its universal platform starting in 2027.

The analyst also believes Rivian is rapidly progressing in its autonomous driving technology, though he notes it still trails leading player Tesla.

All that is to say Perry reinstated coverage of RIVN stock with an Underperform (i.e., Sell) rating, backed by a $14 price objective, implying shares will decline by 6% over the one-year timeframe. (To watch Perry's track record, click here)

Perry says his bearish take could be invalidated should Rivian rollout additional autonomy features, which could increase deliveries – especially point-to-point capabilities later this year – and by stronger-than-expected demand for the R2 given its more affordable $45K price point.

5 other analysts join Perry in the bear camp, but with an additional 8 Buys and Holds, each, the stock claims a Hold consensus rating. Going by the $17.32 average target, a year from now, shares will be changing hands for a 16% premium. (See RIVN stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Stock Futures Fall on Rising Oil Prices amid U.S.-Iran Crisis
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Thursday morning due to rising oil prices amid U.S.-Iran tensions. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.03%, 0.05%, and 0.16%, respectively, at 8:35 a.m. EST on March 5.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

U.S. WTI futures were up 2.8%, trading at $76.81 as of writing. Meanwhile, Brent Crude Oil futures rose more than 2% to $83.20.

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major averages rebounded sharply as investors' war-related anxiety eased. The S&P 500 closed up 0.78%, the tech-heavy Nasdaq added 1.29%, and the Dow rose 0.49%. Technology and semiconductor names led the gains, with Nvidia (NVDA) up 1.7%, Broadcom (AVGO) gaining 1.2%, and Micron (MU), Intel (INTC), and AMD (AMD) each surging over 5%.

Turning to trending stocks, Broadcom (AVGO) shares were up more than 6% in Thursday's pre-market trading after the semiconductor company reported strong fiscal first-quarter results and issued a robust outlook. In contrast, Rigetti Computing (RGTI) stock fell more than 4% after the quantum computing company missed Q4 revenue expectations.

Meanwhile, Trade Desk (TTD) stock rallied more than 21% after The Information reported that the ad-tech platform was in early talks with OpenAI to boost the AI startup's ad business.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Why Is Impact BioMedical Stock (IBO) Up Today?
data.description:
Impact BioMedical (IBO) stock underwent a massive rally on Thursday after the specialty biopharmaceuticals and consumer healthcare company revealed an update to its merger agreement with Dr Ashleys Limited. The deal also included agreements with PubCo, the parent company of Dr Ashleys Limited, Dr Ashleys Bio Labs Limited, and Kanans Visvanats.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This change included an addition to the number of shares that will be issued by PubCo as part of the merger agreement. In addition to the 22,000 shares that will be issued to Impact BioMedical CEO Frank Heuszel, PubCo will issue 53,000 and 75,000 shares to DSS, Inc., (DSS) across two batches. DSS, Inc., is a large shareholder of IBO stock, with nearly 93 million shares held between it and DSS BioHealth Security, Inc., representing an 88.87% stake in Impact BioMedical.

Impact BioMedical also amended the merger completion date to take into account the new changes. The companies now have until July 1, 2026, to complete the merger, compared to the previous deadline of March 31, 2026.

Impact BioMedical Stock Movement Today

Impact BioMedical stock was up 82.72% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a slight rally yesterday. The shares have fallen 18.16% year-to-date and 65.84% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of Impact BioMedical, as some 46 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 174,000 shares.
data.author: William White
